title: Wilson Witzel

infobox.value: Wilson José Witzel  (1968-02-19) 19 February 1968 (age 58)  [Jundiaí](/wiki/Jundia%C3%AD "Jundiaí"), [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo_(state) "São Paulo (state)"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil")
Politician, Lawyer, Professor of Economic Criminal Law

(born 19 February 1968) is a Brazilian politician and lawyer who was the 63rd [Governor](/wiki/List_of_Governors_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "List of Governors of Rio de Janeiro") of the [state of Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/State_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "State of Rio de Janeiro"). A member of the [Partido Democracia Cristã (DC)](/w/index.php?title=Democracia_Crist%C3%A3_(Brasil)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Democracia Cristã (Brasil) (page does not exist)"), Witzel is a former federal judge and is an ex-marine. On 28 October 2018, he was elected Governor of the State of Rio de Janeiro with a four-year term beginning in January 2019, replacing [Luiz Fernando Pezão](/wiki/Luiz_Fernando_Pez%C3%A3o "Luiz Fernando Pezão"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) until his [impeachment](/wiki/Impeachment_of_Wilson_Witzel "Impeachment of Wilson Witzel") in April 2021 for [corruption](/wiki/Corruption "Corruption").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

["Wilson Witzel, do PSC, é eleito governador do RJ"](https://g1.globo.com/rj/rio-de-janeiro/eleicoes/2018/noticia/2018/10/28/wilson-witzel-do-psc-e-eleito-governador-do-rj.ghtml). *G1*. 28 October 2018.

Rosa, PH; Chaves, Raíza (30 April 2021). ["Witzel sofre impeachment e perde o cargo de governador do Rio"](https://noticias.r7.com/rio-de-janeiro/witzel-sofre-impeachment-e-perde-o-cargo-de-governador-do-rio-30042021). *R7* (in Portuguese). Retrieved 30 April 2021.

Godoy, Carol. ["Jundiaiense está no segundo turno para o governo do RJ"](https://tribunadejundiai.com.br/noticias/politica/eleicoes-2018/4853-jundiaiense-esta-no-segundo-turno-para-o-governo-do-rj). *tribunadejundiai.com.br*.

["Wilson Witzel (PSC) - Candidato a Governador, Número 20"](https://web.archive.org/web/20181013014710/https://noticias.uol.com.br/politica/politicos-brasil/2018/governador/rj/19021968-wilson-ex-juiz-federal.htm). Archived from [the original](https://noticias.uol.com.br/politica/politicos-brasil/2018/governador/rj/19021968-wilson-ex-juiz-federal.htm) on 2018-10-13. Retrieved 2018-10-18.
["Wilson Witzel 20 (PSC) Governador | Rio de Janeiro | Eleições 2018"](https://especiais.gazetadopovo.com.br/eleicoes/2018/candidatos/rj/governador/wilson-witzel/). 28 October 2018.

["PSC oficializa candidatura de Wilson Witzel ao Governo do RJ"](https://g1.globo.com/rj/rio-de-janeiro/eleicoes/2018/noticia/2018/07/21/psc-oficializa-candidatura-de-wilson-witzel-ao-governo-do-rj.ghtml). *G1*. 21 July 2018.

Dia, O. (August 6, 2018). ["PSC-RJ anuncia vice na chapa de Wilson Witzel ao Governo do Estado"](https://odia.ig.com.br/rio-de-janeiro/2018/08/5564254-psc-rj-anuncia-vice-na-chapa-de-wilson-witzel-ao-governo-do-estado.html). *O Dia - Rio de Janeiro*.

["Plano de Governo Wilson Witzel Coligação "Mais Ordem, Mais Progresso" PSC – Pros 20 Governo do Estado do Rio de Janeiro - Eleições 2018 - Fonte: Tribunal Superior Eleitoral (TSE)"](https://web.archive.org/web/20181018003156/http://divulgacandcontas.tse.jus.br/candidaturas/oficial/2018/BR/RJ/2022802018/190000612301/proposta_1534218285632.pdf) (PDF). Archived from [the original](http://divulgacandcontas.tse.jus.br/candidaturas/oficial/2018/BR/RJ/2022802018/190000612301/proposta_1534218285632.pdf) (PDF) on 2018-10-18. Retrieved 2018-10-18.

**Wilson José Witzel**  (born 19 February 1968) is a Brazilian politician and lawyer who was the 63rd [Governor](/wiki/List_of_Governors_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "List of Governors of Rio de Janeiro") of the [state of Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/State_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "State of Rio de Janeiro"). A member of the [Partido Democracia Cristã (DC)](/w/index.php?title=Democracia_Crist%C3%A3_(Brasil)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Democracia Cristã (Brasil) (page does not exist)"), Witzel is a former federal judge and is an ex-marine. On 28 October 2018, he was elected Governor of the State of Rio de Janeiro with a four-year term beginning in January 2019, replacing [Luiz Fernando Pezão](/wiki/Luiz_Fernando_Pez%C3%A3o "Luiz Fernando Pezão"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) until his [impeachment](/wiki/Impeachment_of_Wilson_Witzel "Impeachment of Wilson Witzel") in April 2021 for [corruption](/wiki/Corruption "Corruption").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Witzel holds a master's in Civil Law, and has been a professor of Criminal Law for more than 20 years. As a federal judge, he served in different civil and criminal courts in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro") and in Vitória (Espírito Santo).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Wilson_Witzel&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Witzel was born in 1968 in [Jundiaí](/wiki/Jundia%C3%AD "Jundiaí").[[4]](#cite_note-DivulgaCand-4) He spent 17 years acting in civil and criminal courts. He was a tenured federal judge of the 6th Federal Civil Court until 2 March 2018, when he resigned to join the ([PSC](/wiki/Partido_Social_Crist%C3%A3o "Partido Social Cristão")).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

He officially announced his candidacy for the position of Governor of the State of Rio de Janeiro on 21 July 2018.[[6]](#cite_note-6) His deputy was Rio de Janeiro city councilor Cláudio Castro, also of the PSC.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Witzel initially polled at just 1%, tied with André Monteiro ([PRTB](/wiki/Brazilian_Labour_Renewal_Party "Brazilian Labour Renewal Party")), [Dayse Oliveira](/wiki/Dayse_Oliveira "Dayse Oliveira") ([PSTU](/wiki/Unified_Workers%27_Socialist_Party "Unified Workers' Socialist Party")) and Marcelo Trindade ([NOVO](/wiki/New_Party_(Brazil) "New Party (Brazil)")), and was only ahead of Luiz Eugênio Honorato ([PCO](/wiki/Labour_Cause%27s_Party "Labour Cause's Party")). He was behind [Romário](/wiki/Rom%C3%A1rio "Romário") ([PODE](/wiki/Podemos_(Brazil) "Podemos (Brazil)")), [Eduardo Paes](/wiki/Eduardo_Paes "Eduardo Paes") ([DEM](/wiki/Democrats_(Brazil) "Democrats (Brazil)")), [Anthony Garotinho](/wiki/Anthony_Garotinho "Anthony Garotinho") ([PRP](/wiki/Progressive_Republican_Party_(Brazil) "Progressive Republican Party (Brazil)")), [Tarcísio Motta](/wiki/Tarc%C3%ADsio_Motta "Tarcísio Motta") ([PSOL](/wiki/Socialism_and_Liberty_Party "Socialism and Liberty Party")), [Indio da Costa](/wiki/Indio_da_Costa "Indio da Costa") ([PSD](/wiki/Social_Democratic_Party_(Brazil,_2011) "Social Democratic Party (Brazil, 2011)")), [Pedro Fernandes](/wiki/Pedro_Fernandes_Neto_(politician) "Pedro Fernandes Neto (politician)") ([PDT](/wiki/Democratic_Labour_Party_(Brazil) "Democratic Labour Party (Brazil)")) and [Marcia Tiburi](/wiki/Marcia_Tiburi "Marcia Tiburi") ([PT](/wiki/Workers%27_Party_(Brazil) "Workers' Party (Brazil)")).[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Polls closer to the election placed him much higher at 17%, still behind Eduardo Paes, who had 27%, and tied with Romario.[[10]](#cite_note-10) This was considered surprising because Witzel was a relatively unknown in the state and had only 27 seconds of electoral advertising time. Many commentators attributed his popularity to his early endorsement of [Jair Bolsonaro](/wiki/Jair_Bolsonaro "Jair Bolsonaro") [PSL](/wiki/Social_Liberal_Party_(Brazil) "Social Liberal Party (Brazil)").[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

On 7 October 2018, in the first round of the [2018 Rio de Janeiro gubernatorial election](/wiki/2018_Rio_de_Janeiro_gubernatorial_election "2018 Rio de Janeiro gubernatorial election"), Witzel received 3,154,771 votes or 41.28%, coming in first; second was [Eduardo Paes](/wiki/Eduardo_Paes "Eduardo Paes"), of the [DEM](/wiki/Democrats_(Brazil) "Democrats (Brazil)"), former [mayor](/wiki/Mayor "Mayor") of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), with 1,494,831 votes or 19.56%, Tarcísio Motta came third with 10.72%. Witzel and Paes advanced to the second round. The result was considered a surprise given Witzel's low standing in the early polls.

In the second round of the elections, Wilson Witzel was endorsed by the [PRTB](/wiki/Brazilian_Labour_Renewal_Party "Brazilian Labour Renewal Party")'s candidate André Monteiro,[[14]](#cite_note-14) the [Party of the Republic](/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Brazil,_2006) "Liberal Party (Brazil, 2006)") (now known as the Liberal Party),[[15]](#cite_note-15) from PSD candidate Indio da Costa, who initially remained neutral[[16]](#cite_note-16) saying that he did not support Witzel, but later decided to declare his support for the Witzel,[[17]](#cite_note-17) and from [PDT](/wiki/Democratic_Labour_Party_(Brazil) "Democratic Labour Party (Brazil)")'s candidate [Pedro Fernandes](/wiki/Pedro_Fernandes_Neto_(politician) "Pedro Fernandes Neto (politician)").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

On 28 October 2018, in the second round of the gubernatorial election, Witzel obtained 4,675,355 votes or 59.87%. He was thus elected Governor of the State of Rio de Janeiro for a term of 4 years starting January 2019; his opponent, Eduardo Paes, from [DEM](/wiki/Democrats_(Brazil) "Democrats (Brazil)"), received 3,134,400 votes or 40.13%.[[19]](#cite_note-19)[[20]](#cite_note-20)

An ally of president Jair Bolsonaro, Witzel was elected after promising a "slaughter" of drug gangsters. Wilson Witzel officially took office as governor on 1 January 2019.[[21]](#cite_note-21) However, because [Jair Bolsonaro](/wiki/Jair_Bolsonaro "Jair Bolsonaro") took office as [president of Brazil](/wiki/President_of_Brazil "President of Brazil") on the same day, Witzel's ceremony was postponed to 2 January 2019.[[22]](#cite_note-g1-transmissao-de-cargo-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23) For his ceremony, Witzel had a special "governor's [sash](/wiki/Sash "Sash")" made to be handed over to him by then-acting governor [Francisco Dornelles](/wiki/Francisco_Dornelles "Francisco Dornelles"). The practice, however, isn't official in the state of Rio de Janeiro, and wasn't tradition in the state's ceremonies.[[22]](#cite_note-g1-transmissao-de-cargo-22)[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)

In September 2019, following the killing of an eight-year-old girl, hundreds protested in anger in the Complexo do Alemão favela where she was shot, and the hashtag "#aculpaedowitzel" ([Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): *A culpa é do Witzel*, [lit.](/wiki/Literal_translation "Literal translation") 'It's Witzel's fault') led trending topics in Brazil. Cartoons showing the smiling governor wiping blood from his face.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

On 14 April 2020, Witzel tested positive for [COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19")[[27]](#cite_note-27) shortly after on 11 June 2020 an [impeachment process](/wiki/Impeachment_of_Wilson_Witzel "Impeachment of Wilson Witzel") was opened against Witzel alleging graft in purchase of medical supplies.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

On 28 August 2020, the [Superior Court of Justice](/wiki/Superior_Court_of_Justice_(Brazil) "Superior Court of Justice (Brazil)") suspended Witzel from office as a result of suspected fraudulent purchases during the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_Brazil "COVID-19 pandemic in Brazil").[[29]](#cite_note-29)
